channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘They Don’t Need People’: The Workers Left Behind by China’s Robot Drive
item.description: For out-of-work factory workers in Kunshan, a region made rich by electronics manufacturing, a park is the only place to go.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:description: Men looking for daily gigs in Kunshan, the heart of China’s manufacturing industry. As the country pivots toward automation, lower-skilled workers are finding fewer steady opportunities.

item.title: After Being Delayed by Trump, a Canada-U.S. Bridge is Set to Open
item.description: A deal ended the Trump administration’s blocking of a new bridge that will ease congestion at the busiest trade corridor between the United States and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen and Tyler Pager
item.media:description: The Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, in February.

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Remains Absent, a Void at the Top of the Regime
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei did not appear at his father’s funeral this week, fueling speculation about his physical condition and leaving a power vacuum in a divided country.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pro-government demonstration in Tehran in May, with a banner showing Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: At Least 12 People Die in Southern Spain Wildfire
item.description: The blaze in Andalusia, one of the deadliest on record in the country, also left more than 20 people missing. Many of the victims were foreign tourists, the Spanish authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Barragán
item.media:credit: Infoca, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A still from a video provided by the Andalusia Wildfire Service of the wildfire in Los Gallardos district, Spain, on Friday.

item.title: After Venezuela Earthquakes, a Youth Baseball League Counts the Dead
item.description: Children who played for Venezuela’s youth baseball league, a symbol of national pride, have been left injured, orphaned or dead.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, Isayen Herrera and Fabiola Ferrero

item.title: Moscow Still Has Art and Culture. Just Don’t Say ‘Ukraine.’
item.description: An eerie limbo prevails at small art shows, independent theaters and private political clubs, where the war is the elephant in the room.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko and Nanna Heitmann
item.media:description: An art exhibition on a Moscow rooftop, in May.

item.title: Royal Rapprochement: Harry and Meghan Pay a Rare Visit to the King and Queen
item.description: Friday’s visit to King Charles and Queen Camilla signaled a détente in relations with Prince Harry and his wife, who stepped down from royal duties and moved to the United States in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin and Megan Specia
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: Prince Harry visiting a hospital in Birmingham, England, on Thursday.

item.title: Fighting Fires With Figures, These Experts Are Trying to Stop Europe From Burning
item.description: A team of engineers, foresters and scientists is helping the continent prepare for wildfires from a giant science park in Italy. Their arsenal includes satellites, weather models and expert analysis.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.media:description: Firefighters in Patras, Greece, in August. National authorities fight fires within their borders, but the European Union helps coordinate responses and sends additional resources to places in need.

item.title: Mediators Trying to Pull U.S. and Iran Back From Brink, Officials Say
item.description: Qatari mediators were in Iran seeking to salvage the tattered truce. President Trump said talks with Tehran would continue, even if the cease-fire was “over.”
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman, on Thursday.

item.title: Sexual Assault and Killing of Girl Set Off Days of Protest in West Bengal
item.description: After promising to improve safety for women in the Indian state, the party of Prime Minister Narendra Modi is facing public anger after the death of an 11-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: Suhasini Raj and Jeremy W. Peters
item.media:description: Protesting the sexual assault and killing of a young girl in Baruipur, India.

item.title: Greece Arrests 3 in Connection With Bombings Targeting Governing Party
item.description: The attacks killed one person and injured others. The Greek authorities said an investigation would continue.
item.dc:creator: Niki Kitsantonis
item.media:description: Damaged cars and a building in Thessaloniki, Greece, this month after attacks on residential buildings linked to the country’s governing party.

item.title: Can Hungarian Holocaust Survivors Find Justice in the U.S.?
item.description: For more than a decade, a group of survivors have fought a legal battle to get American courts to hear their suit against Hungary’s national railroad over its role in the atrocities.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: The Wall of Remembrance during a memorial service at the Holocaust Memorial Center in Budapest last year.

item.title: Man Arrested on Suspicion of Murdering Ann Widdecombe, Former U.K. Politician
item.description: The police said they had arrested a 26-year-old British man after Ann Widdecombe, a 78-year-old former lawmaker, was found dead with serious injuries on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Ann Widdecombe speaking at a council meeting in Southend-on-Sea, England, in 2022.

item.title: China Launches Reusable Rocket, Clearing a Key Hurdle in Race With SpaceX
item.description: The launch and recovery of the Long March 10B could represent a long-awaited breakthrough for Chinese satellite companies.
item.dc:creator: Selam Gebrekidan and Pei-Lin Wu
item.media:credit: Visual China Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A Long March 10B rocket blasting off from the Wenchang spaceport in Hainan, China, on Friday.

item.title: Meet Neil, Australia’s Celebrity Seal
item.description: The young elephant seal has gained fans around the world. But the authorities warn he could be in danger of being loved to death.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

item.title: Police Investigating Donations to Reform U.K., Reports Say
item.description: The police in London said they had questioned two people as part of an investigation into donations to a political party.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform U.K., during a rally days before the 2024 British general election. He announced his candidacy only weeks earlier.

item.title: U.S. Citizen Tests Positive for Ebola in Democratic Republic of Congo
item.description: The American works for a humanitarian organization in Congo, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Ebola outbreak has killed hundreds of people in the Democratic Republic of Congo this year.

item.title: Ten Killed in Plane Crash on the Bahamas’ Largest Island
item.description: The crash occurred as the country celebrated its independence from Britain.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

item.title: Mexico to File Criminal Complaints in U.S. Over Immigrant Deaths
item.description: The nation’s president vowed to go “beyond diplomatic notes” after a U.S. immigration agent fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican man, in Texas this week.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: A candlelight vigil on Wednesday in Houston, at the spot where Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was shot and killed a day earlier.

item.title: Trump Cuts Habitat Protections for Endangered Species
item.description: The rule change ends a safeguard that had been in place for 50 years and could hasten the demise of imperiled animals.
item.dc:creator: Catrin Einhorn and Maxine Joselow
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Piping plovers need undisturbed beaches to nest and raise their young.

item.title: Museum Spreads 800 Pounds of Peanut Butter in Tribute to Dutch Artist
item.description: An installation honoring the artist, Wim T. Schippers, features a 270-square-foot hexagon spread on the floor of a museum in Rotterdam.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer
item.media:description: Workers at the Museum Boijmans Van Beuningen in Rotterdam, the Netherlands, recreated “Peanut Butter Floor,” an art installation by Wim T. Schippers, last week.

item.title: Ghanaian Influencer Is Extradited Over Romance Scam Targeting Older Americans
item.description: Prosecutors accused Frederick Kumi of running a scheme that defrauded Americans out of $8 million. He was flown to the United States on Thursday, his lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai

item.title: Trump Says Negotiations With Iran Will Continue as U.S. Ramps Up Strikes
item.description: U.S. forces hit more than 170 targets, including air defense systems, drone and missile storage sites, and military speedboats.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: The funeral procession in Tehran on Thursday for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s former leader, who was killed in February during U.S. and Israeli military strikes on his country.

item.title: Passenger Is Partly Sucked Through Window on Ryanair Subsidiary Flight, Reports Say
item.description: A 61-year-old man was being treated for friction burns after a window was damaged during a flight from Greece to Germany. The flight was operated by the Ryanair subsidiary Malta Air.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather, Niki Kitsantonis and Niraj Chokshi
item.media:description: A Ryanair plane at Thessaloniki Airport in Greece.

item.title: Inside a Theater Sheltering Families in Lebanon
item.description: Our reporter Christina Goldbaum visits the Lebanese National Theater in Beirut to meet displaced families who have been sheltering there during the war.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Sutton Raphael

item.title: Indian Diaspora, Now the Largest Foreign-Born Group in Australia, Welcomes Modi
item.description: A large, festive reception for the Indian prime minister in Melbourne belied tensions over migration in the country.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.media:description: People cheering during the Melbourne Meets Modi community event at a stadium in Melbourne, Australia, on Friday.

item.title: Bombing Iran Didn’t Work for Trump. Neither Did a Tentative Cease-Fire. Is There a Plan C?
item.description: The administration appears to be reverting to an all-stick, no-carrot approach. But it has yet to answer why it believes economic warfare and bombing will yield a different result this time.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Monday. The United States has bombed more than 170 Iranian military targets over two nights this week as a cease-fire unraveled.

item.title: Iranian Attacks on Jordan Highlight Kingdom’s Difficult Position
item.description: Iran’s military said it fired missiles at a Jordanian air base used by American forces. Jordan said it had intercepted missiles entering its air space.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A satellite image shows Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Al Azraq, Jordan.

item.title: Albanian Leader Rejects Complaints About Kanye West Concert
item.description: Prime Minister Edi Rama is under fire over a proposed resort with Trump ties, and a planned concert by the controversial musician known as Ye. In both cases, he cites the need for tourism.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: Prime Minister Edi Rama of Albania in Tirana, Albania, in June.

item.title: As U.S. Steps Up Attacks on Iran, Prospect of All-Out War Rises
item.description: American officials said they were trying to safeguard maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran said it had responded by firing at U.S. bases in Qatar, Bahrain and Kuwait.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Yeganeh Torbati, Eric Schmitt and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: The Iranians Who Prefer War
item.description: The latest escalation of hostilities over the Strait of Hormuz is empowering Iranian hard-liners
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Top Iranian officials in Tehran last week.

item.title: Israel Investigating After Photo of Stripped and Bound Detainee Is Shared
item.description: The Israeli military confirmed the authenticity of the photo, which circulated widely after it was shared by a Palestinian activist. Israeli rights groups said it depicted a war crime.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: Russian Finances Get Shakier, Despite Short-Term Bonus From Iran War
item.description: As oil prices fall back to levels not seen since before the Middle East war began, the Kremlin will be forced to confront a deepening budget deficit.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ukrainian attacks on oil refineries have created a fuel shortage in Russia. Cars lined up at a filling station in Anapa, Russia, on Wednesday.

item.title: Palestinian Authority Sets Date for First Major Elections in 20 Years
item.description: Analysts expressed skepticism that the vote would take place as planned. In 2021, Mahmoud Abbas, the authority’s leader, scheduled and then canceled a similar election.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: Mahmoud Abbas, the president of the Palestinian Authority speaking during a conference in Ramallah, in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, in May. His term ended in 2009, but he has since canceled elections.

item.title: In Exchange of Strikes With Iran, U.S. Lands Most of the Blows
item.description: This difference reflects Iran’s limited firepower and degraded air defenses, and highlights how the war is taking a disproportionate toll on Iranians.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A screen grab from a social media video showing the aftermath of a strike on Wednesday in Kuhestak, Iran.

item.title: Poland Sentences Russian Exile to 7 Years in Prison for Spying
item.description: Igor Rogov, who has publicly opposed the Kremlin, was arrested in 2024 and later confessed to spying for Russian intelligence on regime opponents living abroad.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva
item.media:description: Igor Rogov in the Polish Parliament building.

item.title: Iranian port cities and a railway were struck overnight.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and James McManagan
item.media:description: Social media, via Reuters

item.title: Gaza Aid Worker on His Way to Watch World Cup Killed by Israeli Strike
item.description: The Israeli military said it had struck a Hamas militant but was aware of reports that “uninvolved civilians” were harmed.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Abu Bakr Bashir
item.media:description: Mourners carrying the body of Mohammed al-Waheidi, an aid worker killed in an Israeli strike on Tuesday, during his funeral, in Gaza City, on Wednesday.

item.title: Ukrainians Cautiously Welcome Trump’s Announcement About Patriot Missile Systems
item.description: President Trump said the United States would license Ukraine to produce Patriots, which can intercept ballistic missiles. But it could be months or years before those are ready.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd and Oleksandr Chubko
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine meeting with President Trump on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, on Wednesday.

item.title: Factory Blaze in China’s ‘Shoe Capital’ Kills 28
item.description: The country’s leader, Xi Jinping, called on the authorities to investigate the fire, the latest of several deadly workplace accidents in China this year.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo, Pei-Lin Wu and Pranav Baskar
item.media:description: In an image released by Chinese state-run news media, firefighters responded at the scene of a shoe factory fire in Jinjiang, a city in southeastern China, on Thursday.

item.title: I.U.C.N. Update Says Deep-Sea Mining Threatens Mollusks Around Hydrothermal Vents
item.description: The snails and other mollusks around hydrothermal vents have evolved to thrive in extreme conditions, but mineral extraction could drive more than half to extinction.
item.dc:creator: Catrin Einhorn
item.category: International Union for Conservation of Nature

item.title: War-weary Gulf Arab countries are hit by new Iranian attacks.
item.description: Iran
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.media:description: Manama, Bahrain’s capital, in February. The country hosts a U.S. military base.

item.title: As France and Morocco Take the Field, Colonial Past and Global Present Will Mix
item.description: History hangs over the World Cup quarterfinal between France and its former protectorate. These days, national teams intermingle and overlap.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.media:description: Morocco fans gathered in Boston on Wednesday, before their match against France.

item.title: For Ukraine, Other Patriot Makers Are a Cautionary Tale
item.description: Two American allies, Germany and Japan, already have permission to build the American interceptors, a license that President Trump says he will also grant to Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley, Javier C. Hernández and Christopher F. Schuetze
item.media:description: Japan’s defense minister, Shinjiro Koizumi, second right, inspecting a Patriot missile interceptor unit in Tokyo in December.

item.title: Final Stage of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s Funeral Begins After Delay
item.description: Thousands gathered for the procession in Mashhad for the supreme leader, who was killed in the first U.S.-Israeli attacks of the war. Hours earlier, the United States and Iran traded strikes.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times

item.title: A Landfill in Indonesia Caught Fire Over a Week Ago. It’s Still Burning.
item.description: Toxic fumes have sickened nearby residents. Lawmakers and activists say the blaze is an indication of the country’s longstanding issues with waste management.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Rin Hindryati
item.media:credit: Ulet Ifansasti for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke billowed at the Jatiwaringin landfill near Jakarta on Friday as firefighters tried to extinguish the blaze.

item.title: Trump’s NATO Summit, and Everyone Else’s
item.description: While President Trump insulted allies and demanded loyalty, the military alliance moved quietly closer to accepting more European responsibility for defense.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.media:description: President Trump and the leaders of other NATO countries in Ankara, Turkey, on Wednesday.

item.title: Arrest in Minnesota Pandemic Fraud Case Faces Extradition Hurdles in Somalia
item.description: The Trump administration said detaining Abdikerm Eidleh in Somalia was a turning point in the yearslong investigation. But nearly two weeks later, he still hasn’t been brought to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg, Hussein Mohamed and Ernesto Londoño
item.media:description: Police officers in Mogadishu, Somalia, last year. Abdikerm Eidleh, who has been described by U.S. officials as a central figure in the Minnesota pandemic fraud scandal, was arrested in Somalia last month.

item.title: Sifting Through Venezuela Earthquakes’ Rubble, and the Ashes of a Revolution
item.description: The devastation of Jacqueline Zúñiga’s city in Venezuela follows the demise of her political project. The revolution is over, but the human ties that help her cope remain.
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kurmanaev and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
item.media:description: Jacqueline Zúñiga, 52, between several public housing complexes that collapsed during the twin earthquake. Ms. Zúñiga’s building was damaged and she is living in a makeshift shelter.

item.title: Between Trump and Pope, an Ambassador Is Clear About His Allegiance
item.description: Ambassador Brian Burch must navigate the relationship between the two most prominent American men in the world. His priority is the one in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Dias and Motoko Rich
item.media:credit: Stephanie Gengotti for The New York Times
item.media:description: Brian Burch, the U.S. ambassador to the Holy See.

item.title: Mexico Investigates Possible F.B.I. Role in El Mayo Arrest
item.description: Accusations that the United States lied about its involvement in the notorious drug lord’s capture renewed controversy over an issue that has strained the U.S.-Mexico relationship.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.media:description: The notorious drug lord Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada García, was transferred to the United States on a Beechcraft King Air plane in 2024.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Yeganeh Torbati, Ismaeel Naar and Eric Schmitt

item.title: Why India Has Spent Years Blocking the Release of Diljit Dosanjh’s ‘Satluj’
item.description: Censors demanded 127 cuts to the film that explores a dark period of police brutality in Punjab state. Filmmakers say it’s the latest, chilling example of entrenched censorship.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal and Pragati K.B.
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: Tanker Attacks Risk Overplaying Iran’s Hand, Analysts Say
item.description: Iran’s history shows it is willing to dig in, even when it may suffer big losses.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels on the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday. Traffic last weekend was about one-third of the prewar level of more than 100 ships daily, split evenly between the Iranian and Omani sides of the waterway.

item.title: Divisions Festered Within Iran Over Talks With the U.S.
item.description: Iran’s president and foreign minister were physically attacked this week by supporters of a hard-line faction that vehemently opposes any deal with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners gathered in Tehran, Iran, to pay their respect to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei for his funeral on Saturday.

item.title: At the Ayatollah’s Funeral
item.description: A Times reporter in Iran said the events “surpassed anything I’ve ever witnessed.”
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
